creator: Vimarsh Patel
source_text:
Meals for Weight-loss: Ep 9
Tandoori Chicken Rice Bowl

If you want all my recipes with macros 
Comment “Recipe” and I’ll send you the link!!

Instructions

1. Marinate the Chicken
In a large bowl, combine ½ cup Greek yogurt, 1 tbsp oil, 1 tbsp ginger garlic paste, juice of ½ lemon, 2 tsp Kashmiri chili powder, 1 tsp paprika, 1½ tsp garam masala, 1½ tsp cumin, 1 tsp coriander, ½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp salt, and ½ tsp black pepper. Add the 800 g chicken breast and coat well. Marinate for at least 1 hour, or overnight for the best flavor.

2. Cook the Rice
Rinse 2 cups basmati rice until the water runs clear. Add it to a rice cooker with 3 cups water, 1 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp salt, and 1 tsp ghee. Cook using the normal white rice setting.

3. Cook the Chicken
Preheat the air fryer to 400°F (205°C). Air fry the chicken for 12 to 15 minutes, flipping halfway, until it reaches an internal temperature of 165°F (74°C). Let it rest for 5 minutes, then slice.

4. Make the Pickled Onions
In a bowl, combine the thinly sliced red onion, juice of 1 lemon, ½ tsp salt, and ½ tsp red chili powder. Toss well and let sit for at least 20 minutes.

5. Make the Green Chutney
Blend 1 cup Greek yogurt, 1 cup cilantro, ½ cup mint, 4 green chilies, 1 tsp cumin, ½ tsp salt, and 2 tbsp water until smooth. Add a little more water if you prefer a thinner consistency.

6. Assemble
Divide the rice, sliced chicken, pickled onions, and green chutney evenly between 4 meal prep containers.

The goal is 40lbs in 90 days 
I am down 20lbs in 55 days! 

#homecooking #easyrecipe #chicken #tandoori #weightloss macrofriendly amateurprochef

notes: Agent-labeled from the frozen Instagram description before viewing parser output.
The description embeds ingredient amounts inside prose instructions but provides no raw ingredient list, so this is intentionally an incomplete fallback case.
Accepted after general abbreviated-series metadata, explicit divide-among-containers yield, and amount-near-line-start ingredient context rules; no creator identity or exact recipe phrase is used.
